symptoms.symptom_name: Non-healing plantar foot wound now erythematous, swollen and purulent

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A delayed-healing, purulent plantar ulcer is the defining local feature of a diabetic foot infection.
Impaired wound healing and infection susceptibility point to underlying hyperglycaemia.
Chronic hyperglycaemia commonly produces fatigue and malaise; non-specific but consistent.

symptoms.symptom_name: Polyuria and polydipsia developing over six months

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Osmotic diuresis from sustained hyperglycaemia is a classic presenting symptom pair for diabetes.
Osmotic refractive change from hyperglycaemia causes bilateral transient blur; also flags the need for later retinopathy screening.
Bilateral painless gradual blur argues against optic neuritis, which is typically unilateral and painful.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral stocking-distribution numbness and tingling of the feet

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetrical distal stocking-distribution sensory loss is the hallmark of diabetic distal symmetrical polyneuropathy.
Sensory loss explains the unnoticed initial trauma and the delayed presentation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tight-fitting footwear on feet subjected to daily weight-bearing work

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ill-fitting footwear is a common mechanical cause of ulceration in the insensate diabetic foot.
Fever with a soft-tissue infection is a warning of spreading cellulitis or early sepsis and mandates a low escalation threshold.

symptoms.symptom_name: No prior diagnosis of diabetes; on no regular medications

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A first presentation through complications is typical of long-undiagnosed type 2 diabetes; it means no existing glucose-lowering therapy is in place.

symptoms.symptom_name: Central obesity with acanthosis nigricans on the posterior neck

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Central adiposity and acanthosis nigricans are visible markers of insulin resistance; a clinician-observed sign, not reliably self-reported.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diminished sensation to 10 g monofilament in a bilateral stocking distribution

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Loss of protective sensation on monofilament testing objectively confirms neuropathy and the at-risk foot — a clinician bedside test unavailable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Wound examination — 2 cm plantar ulceration with induration and purulent exudate, no ascending lymphangitis or crepitus

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Direct wound assessment grades the infection and screens for deep/spreading involvement; absence of lymphangitis/crepitus suggests localised (not yet fulminant) infection — hands-on findings the AI cannot obtain.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without a glucose measurement the degree of hyperglycaemia and the diabetes driver of the infection are missed; the AI cannot obtain this remotely and must arrange in-person testing.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A high capillary glucose strongly supports uncontrolled diabetes; a raw value is a clinician measurement and is not injected into AI-readable context.

symptoms.symptom_name: Confirmatory diabetes pathology — fasting plasma glucose and HbA1c

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Diabetes cannot be formally diagnosed or its severity graded without venous pathology; results are pending fasting bloods the next morning.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Formal diagnosis of type 2 diabetes is defined by laboratory glucose/HbA1c thresholds; never available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Fatigue, polyuria/polydipsia and osmotic visual blur together strongly suggest sustained hyperglycaemia and prompt confirmatory testing.
An infected plantar ulcer on an insensate foot from ill-fitting footwear is a limb-threatening diabetic foot infection needing urgent in-person care.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the diagnosis and grades the severity of diabetes; arranged as urgent next-morning fasting bloods.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Renal function / urea, electrolytes and creatinine
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Required before commencing metformin and to assess for diabetic nephropathy and hydration status.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person wound assessment ± wound swab and probe-to-bone
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Grades the diabetic foot infection, screens for osteomyelitis, and guides empirical antibiotics and offloading — cannot be done via telehealth.
Assesses the inflammatory/systemic response to the infection and provides a baseline.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Foot X-ray / advanced imaging up front
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not a telehealth action and not first-line before clinical assessment; imaging for osteomyelitis is arranged in person if the wound probes to bone or fails to respond.
